name: Adversarial citation — contradictory findings, source-specific querying

setup.description: Two papers with CONFLICTING results: TOPCAT (negative, P=0.14) and Aldo-DHF (positive on diastolic function, negative on symptoms). Tests that the system correctly presents the conflict without fabricating consensus, and that source-specific queries do not cross-cite data.

papers.title: TOPCAT: Spironolactone for Heart Failure with Preserved Ejection Fraction
Aldo-DHF: Spironolactone in Diastolic Heart Failure

mockChunks.text: Spironolactone did NOT significantly reduce the primary composite endpoint of cardiovascular death, aborted cardiac arrest, or hospitalization for heart failure (HR 0.89; 95% CI 0.77-1.04; P=0.14).
3445 patients aged ≥50 years with symptomatic heart failure and ejection fraction ≥45% were enrolled. The median follow-up was 3.3 years.

A post-hoc regional analysis revealed significant heterogeneity: patients in the Americas (excluding Russia/Georgia) showed a significant benefit (HR 0.82; P=0.026), while patients in Russia/Georgia showed no benefit (HR 1.10; P=0.46).

Spironolactone significantly improved diastolic function as measured by E/e' ratio (change: -1.5 vs -0.1; P<0.001). However, spironolactone did NOT improve symptoms, exercise capacity, or quality of life.
422 patients with diastolic dysfunction and preserved ejection fraction (LVEF ≥50%) were enrolled. Patients received spironolactone 25mg daily for 12 months.

queries.query: What did the TOPCAT trial find about spironolactone in heart failure? Was it effective?
expectedBehavior: Cites [1] for TOPCAT negative result (HR 0.89, P=0.14)
failurePatterns: Cites E/e' ratio data as TOPCAT findings

queries.query: Do these two trials agree on whether spironolactone works in HFpEF? Summarize the conflict.
expectedBehavior: Does NOT fabricate a reconciliation or pooled result
failurePatterns: Fabricates a meta-analysis or pooled result

queries.query: Based on the Aldo-DHF trial specifically, should clinicians prescribe spironolactone for symptom relief in HFpEF?
expectedBehavior: Does NOT cite TOPCAT data to answer this question
failurePatterns: Cites TOPCAT data for Aldo-DHF question

qualityCriteria: TOPCAT negative result (P=0.14) is clearly stated
Aldo-DHF dissociation (function vs symptoms) is explained
Conflict between trials is honestly presented
Source-specific queries use only the relevant trial's data
Post-hoc regional analysis is appropriately caveated
